concerns.text: Task T024 implements 'post_hoc_tukey_hsd' but does not explicitly mandate operating on the categorical noise bins (Low/Moderate/High) defined in FR-002. The spec requires pairwise comparisons between these specific bins. A generic Tukey implementation on continuous noise values would violate FR-005 and the acceptance scenario in US-2, constituting a semantic weakening of the requirement.

Task T025 implements multiple-comparison correction but fails to specify the scope of hypotheses to be corrected. FR-008 and US-2 require correction for the set of pairwise bin comparisons (FR-005) and potentially the likelihood-ratio test (FR-004). Without explicit scoping, the task risks applying correction to the wrong p-values or omitting the primary non-linear hypothesis test, violating the family-wise error control requirement.

Success Criterion SC-001 requires measuring the proportion of retained participants against a target of ≥85% of the recruited sample. The current tasks (T015-T019) implement the filtering logic and schema validation but lack a specific task to calculate this retention metric, compare it to the 85% threshold, and report it as evidence. The acceptance criterion is dropped from the verification steps.

Success Criterion SC-005 requires measuring the model convergence rate against a ≥95% success threshold across primary and sensitivity sweeps. Task T027 only flags warnings if >5% fail. It does not include a task to aggregate convergence rates, calculate the specific percentage, and verify it meets the 95% threshold. This drops the acceptance criterion for SC-005.

Success Criterion SC-002 requires reporting the effect size (Cohen's f²) and 95% confidence interval for the quadratic term. Tasks T022 and T023 focus on fitting the model and performing the likelihood-ratio test (p-value) but do not explicitly include a step to extract, calculate, and format the effect size and confidence interval. This omits a required measurable outcome from the deliverable.
